what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-spec JSON for view A11; it declares the surface identity, title, platform scope, represented node type, status, UI addresses, and build targets for a desktop 'Portals — live transclusion' view.
observations: It is a JSON surface-spec with id 'A11' in area 'A'
Its title is 'Portals — live transclusion' and it is scoped to desktop only
It lists four ui:// addresses: canvas/palette, canvas/portal-window, inspector/versions, toolbar/portal
Its 'build_to' names components canvas, inspector, and toolbar, plus a design-lint command and a tokens source
It explicitly states it was built by a Claude Code session to the listed addresses and that addresses should never be invented
standouts: A portal/transclusion surface has no journeys or cross_journeys despite the journey-spine framing
It binds four distinct UI addresses across canvas, inspector, and toolbar
Status is already 'quality-passed' while sitting in '_unrouted'
Build metadata includes both a lint gate and a token-source mapping
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, and addresses
Uses a ui:// address scheme for UI locations
References a centralized tokens file and a lint command as build-time dependencies
Declares home_journey and journey arrays as part of a routing model
Leaves symbols[] empty when no code symbols are present
concerns: journeys and cross_journeys are empty, which is notable for a surface framed around transclusion
The design_lint command path is relative and its invocation context is not stated
The tokens mapping uses a human-readable arrow notation rather than a machine-friendly path field
Being 'quality-passed' while '_unrouted' may indicate a routing step still pending
notes: A11 is a declared-but-unrouted desktop portal surface whose wiring is captured as four UI addresses plus canvas/inspector/toolbar build targets.
questions: Why does a 'live transclusion' portal have no journeys or cross_journeys?
What concrete UI behavior is referenced by each of the four ui:// addresses?
How and when is the design_lint command executed?
Is the tokens mapping a build-time copy, a transform, or documentation?
What does 'NODE-portal' signify in the type system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to carry the web via journeys/cross_journeys; actual content defines addresses and build targets but leaves those arrays empty, so it is a component-wiring spec awaiting routing.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a desktop portal surface that transcludes content through a portal window, palette, version inspector, and toolbar control, while binding those UI addresses to canvas, inspector, and toolbar components and enforcing design-system consistency.
novelty: The 'live transclusion' framing combined with an address-based wiring spec, plus the inclusion of lint and token sources as first-class build metadata.
contribution: It contributes a concrete surface contract for a portal view: the set of UI addresses, the components it builds into, and the lint/token dependencies that a fused system would keep as a wiring record.
summary_for_embedding: A11.json is a quality-passed, desktop-only surface specification for the 'Portals — live transclusion' view in area A, listing four ui:// addresses and build targets for canvas, inspector, and toolbar components with design-token and lint dependencies, but with empty journeys and cross_journeys.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to record the addressable UI surface and build contract for a live-transclusion portal before routing it into the journey spine.
